subject: Discrete Random Variables
topic: §15 Expected Value of a Discrete Random Variable
question: The distribution function of a discrete random variable $X$ is given by $F(x) = \begin{cases} 0 & x < -2 \\ 0.2 & -2 \le x < 0 \\ 0.5 & 0 \le x < 2.2 \\ 0.6 & 2.2 \le x < 3 \\ 0.6 + q & 3 \le x < 4 \\ 1 & x \ge 4 \end{cases}$ Suppose that $\Pr(X > 3) = 0.1$. (a) Determine the value of $q$? (b) Compute $\Pr(X^2 > 2)$. (c) Find $p(0)$, $p(1)$ and $p(\Pr(X \le 0))$. (d) Find the formula of the probability mass function $p(x)$. (e) Compute $E(X)$.
